last_item: Dashboard — 'Connections' (active/max) shown twice on one screen: a plain Kv row in the Postgres card and a tinted saturation gauge in the Server card; same number, two formats, no extra signal (med/S).
last_result: SHIPPED. Dropped the plain Postgres-card 'Connections' Kv row (count/max + pct, no tint) and kept the single tinted Server-card StatCard gauge (3/100, sub 3%, warn/danger as connPct crosses 75/90, grouped with CPU/Mem/Disk). The kept gauge strictly dominates the deleted row — same numbers plus threshold colour that warns BEFORE the connection limit — so removing the duplicate loses no signal. Postgres card stays coherent and distinct (Status + cache hit, TPS, deadlocks, replication lag — all DB-internal health). −4 rendered lines + an intent comment so it isn't re-added; no new component/control/dep; connPct still consumed by the Server gauge so no dead code. Added a getAllByText('Connections').toHaveLength(1) invariant to Dashboard.test.tsx. Weighed the purist objection (max_connections is a PG *setting* → 'belongs' in the Postgres card) but both Priya (technical) and the heuristics reviewer read connection saturation as a resource-exhaustion symptom (same family as CPU/disk), not a config fact — tinted gauge is the right single home. Panel: 4 SHIP, zero blockers (UX heuristics — kills a simultaneous Consistency + Minimalism violation; Sam — 'two identical numbers made me second-guess whether they meant the same thing; keep the coloured one that warns me'; Priya — 'one metric, one home; the plain row was a dimmer colourless copy'; restraint critic — 'genuine duplicate removal, opposite of over-design; kept gauge strictly dominates'). Gates: typecheck OK, 138 tests OK, build OK (dist regenerated + staged), go build OK (outside sandbox — snap-confine blocks in-sandbox). Next top item: Backups + Settings co-location — the canonical seed item flagged by 4 of 11 audit agents (high/M): bring backup-destination config into /backups so configure-then-run is one place.
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn — rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres — minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
